title: Ord Dorlass

{{Quote|Seeg, the Rodian, and I staged a fistfight in one of the larger clubs in the capital city. The little lizard missed a cue and broke my nose… I threw him over the bar, but it was an 'honest' accident. The stunt worked—when we were released from the local jail the next morning, Tefilous informed us that our popularity on Ord Dorlass had increased by six points.|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
'''Ord Dorlass''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Iskin sector/Legends|Iskin sector]]. Serving as a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depot]] [[Colonist/Legends|outpost]] during the [[Pius Dea Era]], around [[3643 BBY]] it was affected by an [[Unidentified plague|epidemic]] of a dangerous [[Disease/Legends|disease]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] it served as a stopping point in the popular [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] race known as the [[Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade]].

Immediately prior to one such race, when handing a data module over to the [[Racer/Legends|racer]] and undercover [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operative [[Siro Simito]] for secret delivery to the celestial body [[Fait d'Fait]], his fellow Rebel agent [[Kaini]] posed as a [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] from Ord Dorlass. When Simito and his competitor [[Seeg]] stopped on the world during the race itself, they staged a fistfight in a local [[Unidentified nightclub|nightclub]] that resulted in a boost to their popularity.

==Description==
Ord Dorlass was a terrestrial<ref name="CRO" /> [[Planet/Legends|world]]<ref name="CSWE Dorlass">{{CSWECite|II|419|Ord Dorlass}}</ref> located in the [[Ord Dorlass system/Legends|Ord Dorlass system]], a part of the [[Iskin sector/Legends|Iskin sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Trailing Sectors/Legends|Trailing Sectors]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It lay at a distance of no more than seven [[Standard day/Legends|days']] [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel with a Class 1 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] from both<ref name="CRO" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="EAOC" /> celestial body [[Dahvil]] and<ref name="CRO" /> the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]<ref name="EAOC" /> astronomical object [[Tintel]].<ref name="CRO" />

==History==
===Republic history===
Ord Dorlass served as an [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depot]] [[Colonist/Legends|outpost]] for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Pius Dea Era]], a period of [[Time/Legends|time]] that lasted between approximately [[12,000 BBY/Legends|12,000 BBY]] and [[11,000 BBY]], despite it being located outside the borders of Republic [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. When the Republic launched the [[Twelfth Pius Dea Crusade]] against the [[Teirasan]] and [[Zarracine]] [[Species/Legends|species]] in [[11,660 BBY]], [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic forces]] advanced through Ord Dorlass toward the [[Zarracina system/Legends|Zarracina system]].<ref name="EGTW" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
Around [[3643 BBY]],<ref name="Date">Per the reasoning [[Template:TOR_dates|here]], Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]]. Since players of ''The Old Republic'' can assign their companions on the mission "The Terror Plague" independently of the main class storyline at any point during the [[Star Wars video games|game]], the mission must take place at some point around 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> an [[Unidentified plague|epidemic]] of a dangerous [[Disease/Legends|disease]] causing fever and hallucinations took place on Ord Dorlass, and in response quarantine was established. An individual affiliated with one of the sides in the ongoing [[Sith Wars/Legends|struggle]] between the Republic and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] subsequently sent a companion to enter the quarantined area and take samples from the victims of the disease.<ref name="The Terror Plague">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Bionanalysis|The Terror Plague}}</ref>
{{Endgame}}

===Interstellar racing===
{{Quote|The Promenade is one of the shorter races, consisting of only four legs: Dahvil to Ord Dorlass; Ord Dorlass to Tintel; Tintel to Azna; and Azna to Fodro.|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
[[File:Siro_Simito.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The pilot Siro Simito visited Ord Dorlass as part of the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade race.]]
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Ord Dorlass served as the first of the three mid-race stopping points of the popular<ref name="CRO" /> [[Standard year/Legends|annual]]<ref name="CSWE Fodro">{{CSWECite|I|284|Fodro}}</ref> [[Star Rally]] [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] racing route known as the [[Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade]]. At one point, the [[Racer/Legends|racer]] and undercover [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operative [[Siro Simito]] was tasked by the Alliance to deliver a data module from Dahvil to a contact on<ref name="CRO" /> the Mid Rim<ref name="EAOC" /> astronomical object [[Fait d'Fait]] while participating in the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade.<ref name="CRO" />

Similarly to other such missions, the Alliance arranged for the agent [[Kaini]] to hand the data module to Simito. On Dahvil, the starting point of the race, the former agent posed as a [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] from Ord Dorlass who pretended to interview Simito about his feud with the fellow racer [[Seeg]]. As part of the Rebels' pre-arranged act, Simito—pretending to lash out in annoyance at the repeated mentioning of his rival—used his [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-ax]] to slice off the supertelephoto lens of Kaini's [[Cam droid/Legends|cam droid]], and the racer subsequently picked up the casing of the lens, which contained the hidden data module. After the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade began, Simito intentionally decreased the hyperdrive efficiency of his [[Axe (starship)|starship]] on the first legs of the race's route so that he would not later make a suspicious delay on the segment of the route where the racer planned to make a detour to Fait d'Fait.<ref name="CRO" />

During a stop on Ord Dorlass, Simito and Seeg staged for publicity purposes a fistfight at a [[Unidentified nightclub|nightclub]] in the world's [[Unidentified capital city (Ord Dorlass)|capital city]]. In the process, the latter accidentally broke the former's [[Nose/Legends|nose]], and Simito also threw Seeg over the [[Nightclub/Legends|establishment's]] bar. On the next [[Local day/Legends|morning]], after the two racers were relased from the local [[Prison/Legends|jail]] they had been incarcerated at, the Star Rally promoter [[Pann Tefilous]] notified them that their popularity among the audience on Ord Dorlass had risen by six points. At some point by the time that shortly followed the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Rebel Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] included Simito's report, which mentioned the racer's visit to Ord Dorlass during the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade, in a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives.<ref name="CRO" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Quote|I've got relatively few fans on Dahvil, so I was able to walk through the crowd with little incident—until the holovid crew from Ord Dorlass pushes their equipment in front of my face. 'Excuse me, Siro,' the reporter—a Human female wearing blue facepaint—says, 'but we'd like to ask you about your feud with Seeg. Is the bad blood still there?'|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
Ord Dorlass was a suitably colonized world with active entertainment [[Media/Legends|media]].<ref name="CSWE Dorlass" /> The [[Human/Legends|Human]] Rebel Alliance agent Kaini once posed as a reporter forming one half—complemented by her cam [[Droid/Legends|droid]]—of a holovid reporting crew from Ord Dorlass.<ref name="CRO" />

==Locations==
The [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of Ord Dorlass was a cosmopolitan settlement<ref name="CSWE Dorlass" /> that featured multiple nightclubs as well as a jail.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ord Dorlass was created by [[Chuck Truett]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Ord Dorlass [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the world itself, in grid square O-16.<ref name="TEA" />
